title: Đăng ký user
requirement: Đăng ký user với google, github

userStories.title: Register with Google
userStories.story: As an end-user, I want to register using my Google account, so that I can quickly create an account without manually entering my details.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Given I am on the registration page, When I click 'Register with Google' and successfully authenticate with Google, Then my account should be created using my Google email and name.
Given I have already registered with my Google email via GitHub, When I register with Google using the same email, Then my accounts should be merged and I should have a single user profile.
Given I deny Google authentication or an error occurs, When I attempt to register with Google, Then I should see an appropriate error message and my account should not be created.

testCases.title: Successful Google Registration
testCases.steps:
1. Navigate to the registration page.
2. Click 'Register with Google'.
3. Authenticate with a valid Google account.
4. Grant necessary permissions.
testCases.expectedResult: User account is created with Google email and name. User is logged in.

testCases.title: Google Registration with Existing GitHub Email
testCases.steps:
1. Register with GitHub using email X.
2. Log out.
3. Register with Google using the same email X.
4. Complete authentication.
testCases.expectedResult: Accounts are merged. User has a single profile with email X.

testCases.title: Google Registration Denied
testCases.steps:
1. Navigate to the registration page.
2. Click 'Register with Google'.
3. Deny authentication or close the Google login window.
testCases.expectedResult: Registration is not completed. User sees an error message.

userStories.title: Register with GitHub
userStories.story: As an end-user, I want to register using my GitHub account, so that I can quickly create an account without manually entering my details.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Given I am on the registration page, When I click 'Register with GitHub' and successfully authenticate with GitHub, Then my account should be created using my GitHub email and name.
Given I have already registered with my GitHub email via Google, When I register with GitHub using the same email, Then my accounts should be merged and I should have a single user profile.
Given I deny GitHub authentication or an error occurs, When I attempt to register with GitHub, Then I should see an appropriate error message and my account should not be created.

testCases.title: Successful GitHub Registration
testCases.steps:
1. Navigate to the registration page.
2. Click 'Register with GitHub'.
3. Authenticate with a valid GitHub account.
4. Grant necessary permissions.
testCases.expectedResult: User account is created with GitHub email and name. User is logged in.

testCases.title: GitHub Registration with Existing Google Email
testCases.steps:
1. Register with Google using email Y.
2. Log out.
3. Register with GitHub using the same email Y.
4. Complete authentication.
testCases.expectedResult: Accounts are merged. User has a single profile with email Y.

testCases.title: GitHub Registration Denied
testCases.steps:
1. Navigate to the registration page.
2. Click 'Register with GitHub'.
3. Deny authentication or close the GitHub login window.
testCases.expectedResult: Registration is not completed. User sees an error message.

userStories.title: Prevent Duplicate Accounts
userStories.story: As an end-user, I want my accounts to be merged if I register with the same email via Google or GitHub, so that I do not have multiple profiles for the same email.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Given I have registered with Google or GitHub using email Z, When I register with the other provider using the same email Z, Then my accounts should be merged into a single profile.

testCases.title: Merge Accounts on Same Email
testCases.steps:
1. Register with Google using email Z.
2. Log out.
3. Register with GitHub using the same email Z.
4. Complete authentication.
testCases.expectedResult: User has a single profile with email Z. Both Google and GitHub can be used to log in.
